title: Cleaning apparatus with selectable combing unit for removing debris from cleaning roller
abstract: A cleaning apparatus comprising a housing, at least one agitator configured to be rotatably coupled to the housing, a combing unit comprising a plurality of spaced teeth configured to contact the agitator for preventing build up and removing debris, and a switch configured to cause the combing unit to move between an active mode in which the plurality of spaced teeth are configured to contact the agitator for preventing build up and removing debris, and an inactive mode in which the plurality of spaced teeth are configured to not contact the agitator. The switch may be configured to cause the combing unit to rotate about a pivot axis between the active mode and the inactive mode. The switch may be configured to convert linear motion of the switch into the rotational motion of the combing unit about the pivot axis.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A cleaning apparatus comprising: a housing; at least one agitator configured to be rotatably coupled to said housing; a combing unit comprising a plurality of spaced teeth configured to contact said agitator for preventing build up and removing debris; and a switch configured to cause said combing unit to move between an active mode in which said plurality of spaced teeth are configured to contact said agitator for preventing build up and removing debris, and an inactive mode in which said plurality of spaced teeth are configured to not contact said agitator. 2. The cleaning apparatus of claim 1, wherein movement of said switch is configured to cause said combing unit to rotate about a pivot axis between said active mode and said inactive mode. 3. The cleaning apparatus of claim 2, wherein said switch is configured to convert linear motion of said switch into said rotational motion of said combing unit about said pivot axis. 4. The cleaning apparatus of claim 2, wherein said switch includes a switch body configured to move in a slot formed in said housing. 5. The cleaning apparatus of claim 1, further comprising a removable panel configured to be removably coupled to said housing, said removable panel including said combing unit and a slot formed in said removable panel, wherein at least a portion of a switch body of said switch is configured to move in said slot. 6. The cleaning apparatus of claim 5, wherein movement of said switch is configured to cause said combing unit to rotate about a pivot axis between said active mode and said inactive mode. 7. The cleaning apparatus of claim 6, wherein said switch body is configured to move linearly in said slot. 8. The cleaning apparatus of claim 7, and wherein said switch further includes a post configured to extend from said switch body and a cam coupled to said combing unit, wherein said post configured to engage said cam such that linear movement of said switch body in said slot causes said rotational movement of said combing unit about said pivot axis. 9. The cleaning apparatus of claim 9, wherein said cam comprises a generally helical slot coupled to said combing unit, said generally helical slot configured to receive a portion of said post. 10. The cleaning apparatus of claim 5, wherein said slot includes at least one retention region, said retention region configured to maintain said switch in a selected one of said active or inactive modes. 11. The cleaning apparatus of claim 1, wherein movement of said switch is configured to cause said combing unit to move linearly between said active mode and said inactive mode. 12. The cleaning apparatus of claim 1, wherein said switch includes a switch body coupled to said housing, said switch further comprising linkage configured to convert movement of said switch body into movement of said combing unit between said active mode and said inactive mode. 13. The cleaning apparatus of claim 1, wherein said switch further includes an actuator configured to cause said combing unit to move between said active mode and said inactive mode. 14. The cleaning apparatus of claim 13, further comprising a sensor configured to cause said actuator to move said combing unit between said active mode and said inactive mode. 15. The cleaning apparatus of claim 1, wherein said switch further includes a biasing device configured to cause said combing unit to default to one of said active mode or said inactive mode. 16. The cleaning apparatus of claim 15, wherein said biasing device includes a spring. 17. A cleaning apparatus comprising: a housing; at least one agitator configured to be rotatably coupled to said housing; a first panel configured to be removably coupled to said housing, said first panel including a combing unit comprising a plurality of spaced teeth configured to contact said agitator when secured to said housing for preventing build up and removing debris; and at least one of the following: a second panel configured to be removably coupled to said housing, said second panel not including a combing unit; a third panel configured to be removably coupled to said housing, said third panel including a combing unit comprising a plurality of spaced teeth which do not contact said agitator when secured to said housing; or said first panel, wherein said combing unit is configured to move between a first position in which said plurality of spaced teeth are configured to contact said agitator and a second position in which said plurality of spaced teeth do not contact said agitator. 18. The cleaning apparatus of claim 17, comprising said second panel. 19. The cleaning apparatus of claim 17, comprising said third panel. 20. The cleaning apparatus of claim 17, comprising said first panel comprising said combing unit configured to move between said first position and said second position.
